+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-104
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_09-30-08+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs — deleted (dead stub module)
+- crates/io-image/src/lib.rs — removed `pub mod asset_store_stub;` export
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_TASK_2026-05-23_09-22-29+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`: deleted in full via `git rm` (94 lines: `AssetId` type alias, `Cache` trait, `MemoryCache` impl, and two-test `tests` module). This was the stub cache surface that issue #98 / PR #99 flagged as unreachable.
+- `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`: removed the single line `pub mod asset_store_stub;` (line 62). No other text in `lib.rs` referenced the stub, so no further edits were required.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None added or modified. The stub's `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` block (`put_get_roundtrip`, `miss_returns_none`) was removed only as a consequence of deleting the file containing it; no other test file referenced the stub.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None added or modified.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Not needed — the bullet lists above describe both edits in full.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+### Pre-edit gates
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` → clean (no tracked changes).
+- `rg -n "asset_store_stub|rge_io_image::asset_store_stub|crate::asset_store_stub" --glob "*.rs" .` →
+  - `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs:62:pub mod asset_store_stub;`
+  - `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs:11://! \`use crate::asset_store_stub::Cache;\` → \`use rge_asset_store::Cache;\`.`
+  Both expected: the stub file itself and the module export line. No external callers.
+- `rg -n "\b(Cache|MemoryCache|AssetId)\b" --glob "*.rs" .` → inspected. Within `crates/io-image/` all matches are confined to `asset_store_stub.rs`. Outside that crate, all matches resolve to unrelated real types and were confirmed not to reference the stub:
+  - `editor/rge-editor/src/main.rs` — uses `rge_io_gltf::{Cache, MemoryCache}` (a different crate's real cache, not the io-image stub).
+  - `kernel/asset/tests/*.rs`, `kernel/asset-view/src/registry.rs` — use `rge_kernel_asset::AssetId` (kernel-asset's real `AssetId`, distinct from the stub's `Vec<u8>` alias).
+  - Various other crates' own internal `Cache`/`AssetId` types (kernel-asset-view, kernel-asset, etc.) — none import from `rge_io_image::asset_store_stub`.
+  No remaining Rust code refers to the deleted stub's `Cache`, `MemoryCache`, or `AssetId`.
+
+### Post-edit gates
+
+- `rg -n "asset_store_stub|rge_io_image::asset_store_stub|crate::asset_store_stub" --glob "*.rs" .` → **no matches** (Rust-code references fully removed).
+- `rg -n "\b(Cache|MemoryCache|AssetId)\b" --glob "*.rs"` within `crates/io-image/` → **no matches**. Workspace-wide matches outside io-image are unchanged from the pre-edit inventory above and remain confined to unrelated `rge_io_gltf`, `rge_kernel_asset`, and other real types.
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-image --all-targets --no-fail-fast` → exit 0
+  - lib unittests: 16 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+  - tests/mip_chain_test.rs: 5 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+  - tests/round_trip_exr.rs: 2 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+  - tests/round_trip_hdr.rs: 2 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+  - tests/round_trip_jpeg.rs: 3 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+  - tests/round_trip_png.rs: 4 passed / 0 failed / 0 ignored
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0 (`VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.`)
+  - [1] cargo +nightly fmt --check → ok
+  - [2] architecture lints → ok (all 10 enforcement lints PASS; pre-existing io-* `package.metadata.rge.formats` warnings unchanged)
+  - [3] architecture-lint test suite → ok
+  - [4] cargo build --workspace --all-targets → ok
+  - [5] cargo test --workspace --no-fail-fast → ok
+  - [6] cargo test --workspace --doc → ok
+  - [7] cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run → ok
+- `git diff -- crates/io-image/src/lib.rs crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` → source diff is exactly the one-line removal of `pub mod asset_store_stub;` plus the full deletion of `asset_store_stub.rs`. Diff:
+
+  ```
+  diff --git a/crates/io-image/src/lib.rs b/crates/io-image/src/lib.rs
+  --- a/crates/io-image/src/lib.rs
+  +++ b/crates/io-image/src/lib.rs
+  @@ -59,7 +59,6 @@
+       reason = "format-adapter crate: ..."
+   )]
+
+  -pub mod asset_store_stub;
+   pub mod error;
+   pub mod exr;
+   pub mod format_detect;
+  ```
+
+- `git status --short` after writing this EXEC packet:
+
+  ```
+  D  crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs
+   M crates/io-image/src/lib.rs
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_2026-05-23_09-30-08+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_2026-05-23_09-30-08+0300.meta.json
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_TASK_2026-05-23_09-22-29+0300.md
+  ?? ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_TASK_2026-05-23_09-22-29+0300.meta.json
+  ```
+
+  The TASK packet + sidecar were already present (untracked) when the dispatch began — that is the normal orchestrator-handoff state and is outside the Executor's MAY-edit envelope. The only Executor-authored additions are the EXEC packet and its sidecar; the only source changes are the stub deletion and the one-line lib.rs edit.
+
+## Deviations from Task Packet
+
+None — execution stayed strictly within the Task Packet scope.
+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+None. The deletion is mechanical and self-contained; no design judgment was exercised.
+
+## Worktree State
+
+- Tracked files: `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` (deleted, staged via `git rm`), `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs` (modified, unstaged).
+- Untracked items: the TASK packet + sidecar (`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_TASK_2026-05-23_09-22-29+0300.md` and `.meta.json`) carried in from the orchestrator handoff, plus this EXEC packet + its sidecar.
+- Branch: `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-104`
+- Last commit: `91a9241` chore(dispatch): queue task #10 io-image stub deletion
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-104
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---

@@ -0,0 +1,122 @@
+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-104
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_09-22-29+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs
+- crates/io-image/src/lib.rs
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Implement GitHub issue #104 by deleting the dead `rge-io-image` asset-store stub that issue #98 / PR #99 pre-audited as unreachable stub-only code. This dispatch is deletion-only: remove the unused module file and its public module export without replacing it with an adapter or changing image loading/cache behavior.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`
+- `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any dependency declaration, workspace dependency entry, feature flag, or Cargo metadata file.
+- Any Rust source file other than `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` and `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`.
+- Any test file.
+- Any documentation file.
+- Any script, schema, lint configuration, workflow configuration, `.gitignore`, status file, queue/dispatch automation file, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet.
+- Any handoff packet other than the single required `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_*.md` execution report.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_*.md`.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, tests, docs, fixtures, generated artifacts, persisted logs, screenshots, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, or CLOSEOUT packets.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- Delete `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`.
+- Remove only the `pub mod asset_store_stub;` export from `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet under `ai_handoffs/` documenting the exact diff, reference-search evidence, verification results, and final scope check.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` is deleted from the working tree.
+- `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs` no longer contains `pub mod asset_store_stub;`.
+- No replacement asset-store adapter, cache abstraction, image identity, image loading behavior, image cache behavior, tests, docs, or dependency changes are introduced.
+- A repo-wide Rust-code search finds no in-tree references to `asset_store_stub`, `rge_io_image::asset_store_stub`, or `crate::asset_store_stub` after the deletion.
+- Any remaining in-tree Rust-code matches for `Cache`, `MemoryCache`, or `AssetId` are inspected and confirmed not to reference the deleted `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` types. If that cannot be proven, this dispatch must halt.
+- `cargo test -p rge-io-image --all-targets --no-fail-fast` exits 0.
+- `.\\.ai\\dispatch.verify.ps1` exits 0.
+- The final repository diff is limited to:
+  - deletion of `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`
+  - removal of `pub mod asset_store_stub;` from `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`
+  - the single new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_*.md` execution report
+- The execution report footer contains exactly one line-anchored `HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE` marker and routes `NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI` when all gates pass.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This is a deletion-only cleanup dispatch.
+- Do not implement or sketch a real `rge-io-image` asset-store adapter.
+- Do not replace the stub with a compatibility module, re-export, shim, trait, cache wrapper, `AssetId`, or `MemoryCache` equivalent.
+- Do not change image decoding, loading, caching, identity, error handling, public data types, feature flags, or crate dependencies.
+- Do not add tests or modify existing tests unless a fresh Planner packet explicitly expands scope.
+- Do not fix unrelated compile, lint, formatting, dependency, or verification failures.
+- Do not commit, push, merge, open a PR, or add GitHub issue comments.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their
+`EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before editing, expected no tracked changes outside any already-approved dispatch context. If unexpected tracked changes exist, document them and halt rather than cleaning or reverting them.
+- Before editing, run a repo-wide Rust-code reference search for the stub module. Use `rg -n "asset_store_stub|rge_io_image::asset_store_stub|crate::asset_store_stub" --glob "*.rs" .` if `rg` is available; otherwise use an equivalent PowerShell recursive text search over `*.rs` files. Expected matches must be limited to the stub file itself and the `pub mod asset_store_stub;` line in `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`.
+- Before editing, search in-tree Rust code for possible references to stub-defined exported names: `rg -n "\\b(Cache|MemoryCache|AssetId)\\b" --glob "*.rs" .` if `rg` is available; otherwise use an equivalent PowerShell recursive text search over `*.rs` files. Inspect the matches and confirm none refer to the `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` definitions. Matches from other crates' real cache or asset-id types are not blockers when they clearly do not resolve to this stub.
+- After editing, run `rg -n "asset_store_stub|rge_io_image::asset_store_stub|crate::asset_store_stub" --glob "*.rs" .`, expected no Rust-code references.
+- After editing, run `rg -n "\\b(Cache|MemoryCache|AssetId)\\b" --glob "*.rs" .`, inspect the matches, and document that no remaining Rust code refers to the deleted stub's `Cache`, `MemoryCache`, or `AssetId`.
+- Run `cargo test -p rge-io-image --all-targets --no-fail-fast`, expected exit 0.
+- Run `.\\.ai\\dispatch.verify.ps1`, expected exit 0.
+- Run `git diff -- crates/io-image/src/lib.rs crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` and document that the source diff is limited to deleting the stub file and removing the one module export line.
+- Run `git status --short` after writing the EXEC packet, expected changes limited to `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs`, `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs`, and one `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-104_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt (without committing) and request fresh guidance if any
+of the following occur:
+
+- Any in-tree Rust code outside `crates/io-image/src/asset_store_stub.rs` and `crates/io-image/src/lib.rs` references `asset_store_stub`, `rge_io_image::asset_store_stub`, or `crate::asset_store_stub`.
+- Any in-tree Rust code still references the stub-defined `Cache`, `MemoryCache`, or `AssetId`, or the Executor cannot confidently distinguish remaining `Cache` / `AssetId` matches from unrelated real cache or asset-id types.
